title: HTTP Status Codes Reference: Every Code Explained with
description: Think you know every error? Explore all HTTP status codes (1xx-5xx) and discover the hidden reasons your site might be breaking.

acceptedAnswer.text: HTTP status codes are standard responses given by web servers on the internet. They indicate whether a specific HTTP request has been successfully completed, providing crucial context for troubleshooting. They are divided into five categories: informational (1xx), successful (2xx), redirection (3xx), client error (4xx), and server error (5xx).

mainEntity.name: Where can I find a complete HTTP status code list?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can find a complete, searchable list of all standard HTTP status codes right here on this page. We've compiled every official status code from 100 to 511, including explanations, common causes, fixes for error codes, and curl examples to test them.

mainEntity.name: What does a 403 Forbidden status code mean?

acceptedAnswer.text: A 403 Forbidden error means that the server understands your request, but refuses to authorize it. Unlike a 401 Unauthorized code (which means you need to log in), a 403 means you are authenticated, but you do not have the required permissions to access that specific page or resource.

mainEntity.name: How do I fix an HTTP 502 Bad Gateway error?

acceptedAnswer.text: An HTTP 502 Bad Gateway error occurs when a server acting as a proxy or gateway receives an invalid response from an upstream server. To fix it, you should verify that the backend application (like Node.js, Python, or PHP) is running correctly and that the proxy server (like Nginx or Apache) is properly configured to communicate with it.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between a 301 and 302 redirect?

acceptedAnswer.text: A 301 Moved Permanently indicates that a resource has been permanently moved to a new URL, and search engines should update their links. A 302 Found is a temporary redirect, signaling that the resource is temporarily located elsewhere, but future requests should still use the original URL.
